---
description: "Connect performance review guidelines — defines 'impact', 'big rocks', the three impact categories, and writing quality standards. Used when writing a Connect document."
---

# Connect Writing Guidelines

## What Is "Impact"

Impact is any meaningful outcome that resulted from your involvement. Preferably that outcome is measurable — your involvement improved a metric or produced a qualitatively measurable positive change to the business.

A qualitative improvement can be demonstrated through concrete evidence such as positive feedback from a customer or an internal stakeholder.

Not all activities connect to a measurable result, but there may be data or anecdotal evidence that the activity was worth pursuing. Include those activities too, using whatever metrics or anecdotal evidence are available. The value of the activity is implied even when it is not explicitly measurable.

## The "Big Rocks" Principle

Do not list every minor task performed. Think in terms of business impact. Report the significant efforts delivered and describe each item in terms of what big things were done to make a difference to the business. Mention supporting activities only as they reinforce a "big rock" impact item.

## Collaborative Impact

Wherever possible, describe how activities built on the accomplishments of others or contributed to the success of others. Impact is not just individual accomplishment — it leverages the work of others and helps other people or groups have impact.

## Impact Categories

Organize all impact statements under one of the three H2 headings below. Choose the most appropriate category for each item.

---

### Deliver secure, high-quality content for customers

We are our customers' trusted way to learn about Microsoft technologies. As content developers, we deliver accurate, high-quality, and discoverable content ready for human and agentic consumption. That content helps customers solve problems, make informed decisions, and build confidently. We collaborate with subject matter experts across product and partner teams to ensure our content reflects product truth and customer signals. AI is embedded in our day-to-day workflows, enabling us to scale our efforts and maintain quality more efficiently than ever before. Throughout the content lifecycle, we uphold rigorous standards for quality and trustworthiness.

---

### Continuously improve and innovate

We continuously improve how we work to better meet our customers' needs and drive business impact. We drive measurable improvements to our operations and standards, pilot bold experiments to solve our business problems, and scale what works for greater results. We reflect on what we have learned, share learnings with each other, and partner whenever and wherever possible.

---

### Work better together with peers and partners

We collaborate in measurable and meaningful ways with our peers, partners, and stakeholders in support of Learn Content's FY26 focus areas. When working towards our team goals, we act with urgency, join in with others, adapt, and pivot quickly. We connect with each other, step in to help fill gaps, and share our knowledge in ways that can lead to impact for others and foster a "better together" culture.
